difficulty_definitions.L1: Simple CRUD / single view / static content. <150 LOC target. One wizard turn.
difficulty_definitions.L2: Non-trivial glue: state mgmt, 2+ screens, one external API or framework primitive. 1-2 turns.
difficulty_definitions.L3: Framework-heavy or stateful: shaders, audio graphs, ECS, multi-agent, persistent storage, network sync, sensors. 3-4 turns.

avoid_list.note: These categories already have 3+ installed/shipped versions each. Only include a new entry in these areas if it pushes into genuinely new mechanics.
saturated: habit tracker / weather / BMI / unit converter

entries.goal: Kitchen timer mini-app with multiple concurrent timers. Add any number of named timers (e.g. 'pasta 8min', 'eggs 6min'), all tick down simultaneously on the same screen, each plays a distinct chime when done.
entries.notes: Novel-mechanic cousin of pomodoro — many-concurrent-timers is the new element.

entries.goal: Decision maker / coin flip on steroids. Paste options, weight them (1-10), spin a wheel to pick. Keep last 5 decisions with timestamps.
Meeting time finder across timezones. Add attendee cities, show a visual time-band from 6am-10pm per city, highlight the overlap where everyone is in working hours.

Guided weekly review. Structured prompts (3 wins / 3 lessons / 3 next-week commits / energy 1-10 / focus 1-10), save each review, browse history with a sparkline of energy+focus over weeks, export markdown bundle.
Eisenhower matrix task sorter. 2x2 grid (urgent/not-urgent x important/not-important), drag-and-drop tasks between quadrants, color-code by quadrant, quick-add input, persistent.

Kanban with WIP limits. 3 columns (backlog / doing / done), drag cards between, enforce a 'doing' WIP limit (editable per column), red-outline when over limit, cycle-time stat (median days card spent in 'doing').

Mind-map editor with radial layout. Central node, click to spawn child nodes, drag to reposition, edit labels inline, connect distant nodes with curves, zoom and pan with wheel+drag. Export as PNG and as a nested JSON.

Outliner like Workflowy. Infinite-depth bulleted list, Tab/Shift-Tab to indent, Enter for new item, drag to reorder, collapse/expand with click, zoom into a node (its children become the root), breadcrumb trail, full-text search.
Morse code translator + trainer. Type text, see/hear morse (WebAudio beeps at adjustable WPM), decode mode (type dots/dashes, see text). Training mode: hear letter, type it.
NATO phonetic alphabet trainer. Random letter shown, user types the codeword (Alpha, Bravo, Charlie...). Track accuracy over 20 rounds. Also a reverse mode.

Periodic table of elements. 18-column layout, click element to open a rich card (atomic number/mass/config, group/period, 3 interesting facts). Filter by category (alkali metal, halogen, noble gas...), search by name or symbol, color-code by category.
Ear training for intervals. Play two notes (WebAudio), user picks interval name from 8 options (minor 2nd through octave), streak counter, difficulty that introduces more intervals as you level up.
Country/capital quiz with a map. Show an SVG world map (inline, simplified), highlight a random country, user picks capital from 4 options; flag-emoji hint on wrong answers.
ASL fingerspelling trainer. Show letter A-Z as an SVG hand diagram, user types the letter on keyboard, track speed and accuracy, quick-drill mode (20 random letters).

Simple DC circuit simulator. Drag-and-drop components (battery, resistor, LED, switch) onto a grid, connect with wires, run to compute currents with Kirchhoff's laws, light the LED if current > threshold. Reset and clear.
Interactive function plotter. Input f(x) with sin/cos/log/exp, plot on canvas with axes and grid, drag to pan, wheel to zoom, plot up to 3 functions in distinct colors, show intersection markers.

Punnett square + inheritance simulator. Input two parent genotypes (up to 3 gene loci, e.g. AaBbCc), generate the full Punnett grid, show phenotype ratios, run a Monte-Carlo of 1000 offspring with a histogram.
Lights Out puzzle. 5x5 grid of buttons, clicking flips the button and its orthogonal neighbors, goal is all off. Move counter, randomize with a solvable preset.
Simon says memory game. 4 colored/toned quadrants, sequence grows each round, user repeats, fail = game over, best round saved.
Sokoban puzzle. 5 built-in levels, arrow keys move the pusher, push crates onto target tiles, can't pull, undo button, level complete when all crates on targets, move counter.

Bubble-shooter puzzle. Grid of colored bubbles at top, shooter at bottom aims by mouse, matching 3+ same-color pops and drops disconnected clusters. Score, next-bubble preview, game-over on reach-bottom.
Tangram puzzle. 7 classic pieces drawn as polygons, drag to move, click to rotate in 15° increments, target silhouette on the right. Snap-to-fit tolerance, 5 built-in silhouettes.

Bridge-building physics puzzle. Draw beams between fixed anchor points, click 'run' to drop a weight, beams stress-colored (green/yellow/red) and break under too much force. 3 levels, budget constraint on total beam length.

Tower defense. Fixed path, 3 waves of 10 enemies, 3 tower types (cannon/archer/ice-slow) placeable on grid, money-per-kill, upgrade cost scales per level, base has 20HP. Keep everything in one HTML file.

Auto-battler mini-game. Between rounds, buy units from a 5-slot shop (cost scales by tier), drag to your 4-slot lineup, combine three-of-a-kind into tier-up. Battle phase auto-resolves left-vs-right with simple HP/ATK, 8 rounds.

Falling-sand cellular automaton. Canvas with pixel-size grid, palette of materials (sand, water, stone, fire, plant, lava, oil), click-drag to paint, each frame runs the physics (sand falls, water flows, fire spreads, plant grows on dirt+water, etc.).
Color palette generator. 5-swatch harmonious palettes (complementary / analogous / triadic toggle), click to copy hex, lock swatches that survive regeneration, export as CSS variables.
Haiku writer with live 5-7-5 syllable counter per line. Save haiku gallery to localStorage, export as a nicely-laid-out PNG card.
Pixel-art editor. 32x32 canvas, pen/eraser/bucket-fill/eyedropper tools, 16-color palette, undo stack (20 steps), import-from-image (nearest-neighbor resize), export PNG at 1x and 8x scale.

16-step drum machine. 4 tracks (kick/snare/hi-hat/clap) synthesized with WebAudio (no samples), tap steps to toggle, play/pause, tempo slider 60-200 BPM, swing slider. Save/load patterns to localStorage.
Spirograph drawing. Adjustable outer-radius / inner-radius / pen-offset sliders, rainbow-hue option, pen-width slider, export PNG, save gallery of drawings.
Markdown-to-slides. Split the markdown on '---' lines to make slides, arrow keys to navigate, 'F' for fullscreen, code blocks syntax-highlighted, slide N of M indicator, progress bar.

Polyphonic WebAudio synth. Click piano keys or A-L keyboard, 8-voice oscillator bank with saw/square/sine toggle, ADSR envelope sliders, low-pass filter with resonance, stereo delay, visual waveform scope.
Interactive fluid-dynamics background. Stable-fluids or Navier-Stokes lite on a grid, mouse push/pull, color field advects, 60fps at 512x512. Pure WebGL fragment shader preferred.

Three.js mini-scene (loaded from CDN). Rotating model on a plane, orbit-controls camera, 3 lights (ambient + directional + point), shadow casting, material switcher (basic / lambert / phong / normal), background skybox.
Generative poster tool. Pick a style (bauhaus / risograph / brutalist / wavy), seed number, generates an abstract poster with title text. Export high-res PNG (2400x3200). Regenerate button reseeds.
QR-code generator. Type text or URL, see the QR live, adjustable error-correction level, download PNG. Pure client-side, no external service.
entries.notes: L1 fast-path timed out 2026-04-24. Hypothesis: single-API-call budget isn't enough to inline a working QR encoder — Reed-Solomon + bitmatrix is too much for one shot. Candidate rework: split into (a) CDN-loaded qrcode.js variant (cheap) and (b) pure-JS variant (L2 or L3). Until reworked, rotation should deprioritize this entry.

entries.goal: Numeric base converter. Input in any of bin/oct/dec/hex, the other three update live. Bitwise operations (AND/OR/XOR/NOT/shift) row below with two inputs and a result.
Tabletop dice roller. Roll notation input (e.g. '3d6+2', '1d20', '4d8-1'), parses and rolls, shows individual dice + total, history of last 20 rolls.
Regex playground. Pattern + flag toggles (g/i/m/s/u), test textarea with live-highlighted matches, capture-group table per match, common-pattern quick-pick (email/url/ipv4/uuid).
Side-by-side JSON diff viewer. Paste two JSON blobs, structurally diff (not textual), highlight added keys green / removed red / changed yellow / unchanged grey. Copy-the-diff-as-path-list button.

WCAG contrast-ratio checker. Two color inputs (foreground + background with color pickers), live ratio number, AA/AAA pass/fail badges for normal and large text, color-blindness simulation preview (protanopia / deuteranopia / tritanopia).
Cron expression visualizer. Input a cron string, plain-English explanation, next-10-fires list as timestamps, calendar heatmap showing firing density over the next 30 days.

entries.goal: SQLite playground. Load sql.js from CDN, textarea for SQL, run button, results in a sortable table. Schema explorer panel (tables → columns → types). Preloaded with 3 sample tables (employees, orders, products). Save/load queries to localStorage.
Markdown IDE mini-app. Multi-document sidebar, live preview with auto-generated table of contents, slash-commands (/todo, /code, /quote, /hr), auto-save per doc, export ZIP of all docs as .md files.

REST API explorer. Input URL + method + headers + body, send through mchatai.com proxy, show status / headers / body (pretty-JSON or raw) / elapsed ms. History of last 20 requests, name-and-save-as-snippet, cURL-copy.
entries.notes: Exercises u-020 proxy-fetch rule.

entries.goal: Fair-tip splitter. Each diner enters what they ordered (price), shared items can be split evenly or by weight. Configurable tip and tax, each person sees their personal total including fair share of shared items + prorated tip/tax.
entries.notes: Twist on tip calc — per-person share allocation is novel.

entries.goal: Loan amortization. Principal / APR / term-years inputs, show monthly payment, total interest, and a full amortization table (month / principal / interest / balance). 'Extra payment per month' slider that shows interest saved.

Envelope budgeting. Define monthly envelopes with allocations summing to income, log transactions against an envelope, show remaining per envelope with a progress bar (red when over), rollover unspent on month change.

Portfolio rebalance calculator. Enter current holdings (ticker / shares / price), target allocation percentages, the app tells you how many shares of each to buy/sell to reach targets with the minimum number of trades. Save portfolios locally.
FIRE (financial independence) calculator. Current savings, monthly contribution, expected return rate, annual spend, show years-to-retirement, visualize with a line chart of net worth over the years.

Stock watchlist. Add tickers, fetch quotes via mchatai.com proxy (free Stooq-style endpoint), show current price / day-change / 30-day sparkline per ticker, total portfolio value if you store share counts. Persist watchlist locally.
entries.notes: Proxy-fetch + real-data path.

entries.goal: Options payoff diagram. Build a multi-leg strategy (buy/sell call/put with strike and premium), plot profit/loss vs underlying at expiry, greeks panel (delta / gamma / theta / vega at current spot), save/load strategies.
Water-intake tracker with streaks. Tap a glass icon +1, daily goal editable, 14-day streak grid (green if met / red if missed), gentle haptic-like pulse animation on tap.
Box-breathing guide. Animated square that expands (4s inhale), pauses (4s hold), contracts (4s exhale), pauses (4s hold). Session-length 1/3/5/10 min, subtle WebAudio chime at phase changes.
Sleep journal. Log bedtime/wake-time per day, compute hours and sleep-debt vs 8h target, 30-day bar chart, average and stdev stats, note-field per night.

Posture-break timer. Configurable interval (30/45/60 min), when it fires show a 30-second stretch guide (cycle through 4 stretches with images or emoji). 'Done' button dismisses; 'snooze 5' postpones.
Simple food log. Pick from a built-in DB of 80 common foods (kcal / protein / carbs / fat per 100g), log portion in grams, daily totals with a macro donut chart, configurable goal numbers.
Strength log with 1RM curves. Define exercises, log sets as reps×weight per session, compute 1RM estimate (Epley formula), chart 1RM over time per exercise, volume chart per week, exercise templates.
Quick group poll. Enter question + 2-6 options, shareable URL that encodes poll in the hash, live bar-chart results updated on click (single-device, no backend).
Daily gratitude journal. Three prompts per day ('what went well', 'who helped you', 'what you're looking forward to'), browse history by calendar, gentle reminder at configurable time.
Wedding seating planner. Define tables (capacity), drag guests onto tables, maintain a 'do-not-seat-together' list that shows warnings, print-friendly export view.
Secret Santa drawer. Enter participants and exclusions (e.g. couples can't draw each other), run the match algorithm, reveal each person's pick via a one-click private link they open alone.
Tournament bracket maker. Enter 4/8/16 competitors, single-elimination bracket rendered as a tree, tap a match to set the winner, auto-advance, export bracket as PNG.

Group-trip expense splitter. Add trip members, log expenses (who paid, who owes, equal or custom split), settle-up view computes minimum transactions to balance, shareable URL that encodes the trip state.

ASCII-art webcam. Request webcam permission, live video downsampled to a character grid using a luminance ramp (' .,:;xX$'). Adjustable character size and density, invert toggle, freeze-frame + copy-text.
Regex railroad-diagram visualizer. Parse a regex, render as a classic railroad diagram SVG, hover parts to see what they match. Support common JS regex features.
Turing machine visualizer. Tape with input, state-transition table editor, step/run/pause, highlight current cell and state, 3 built-in programs (increment / binary-to-unary / palindrome-check).

Markov-chain text generator. Paste seed text, train an order-2 Markov model client-side, generate paragraphs in that style, adjust temperature and length, export outputs. Several preset corpuses (Shakespeare / Lovecraft / Hemingway opening).

Epoch/timezone converter. Input unix epoch (seconds or ms auto-detect) or ISO-8601, see converted to 6 timezones (UTC/NYC/LA/London/Tokyo/Sydney). Reverse: pick date + TZ, get epoch. Copy buttons per row.

macOS menubar clock app. Native SwiftUI, Package.swift project, builds with `swift build`. Shows current time in big digits when popover opens. Click the menubar item to toggle. No preferences window needed.

entries.goal: macOS color-contrast checker. Two ColorPicker wells (foreground + background), live WCAG contrast ratio displayed, AA/AAA badges for normal and large text. Native SwiftUI, no network.

entries.user_outcome: GOOD first beta — 44KB artifact, all 6 mechanics wired (parallax/landers/mutants/bombers/rescue/radar/smart-bomb)
entries.goal: Classic Defender-style side-scrolling arcade shooter. Horizontal-scrolling planet surface (parallax mountains on a canvas), player ship with thrust + fire + smart-bomb. Enemies: landers (try to abduct humans from the surface), mutants (faster, spawned when lander reaches top of screen carrying human), bombers (drop mines). Rescue mechanic: if you shoot a lander carrying a human, catch the human as it falls and carry it back to the surface. 5 waves, wave counter, score with multipliers, radar mini-map at top showing enemy + human positions on the full scrollable world (world is wider than viewport). All in one HTML file.
entries.notes: Requested 2026-04-24 — user wants meatier arcade territory. Defender is the canonical 'too-ambitious' arcade clone (parallax scrolling, world-larger-than-viewport, radar, rescue mechanic, AI states). Great stress-test for whether L3 can hold scope across 4 turns. Novel vs. existing catalog (no shooters yet).

entries.user_outcome: GOOD first beta — 34KB artifact, plays. UFO + split-on-hit may be partial (run @ 236s, well under L3 budget, so room to refine).
entries.goal: Asteroids arcade game. Triangle ship with rotation + thrust + wrap-around-screen + fire. Asteroids split into smaller ones when hit (large → 2 medium → 2 small → destroyed). UFO that appears periodically and shoots back. Score by asteroid size, extra life at 10000 points, 3 lives to start. Thrust leaves particle trail. All in one HTML file.
entries.notes: Classic shooter. Exercises vector math (rotation + thrust) and split-on-hit entity spawning. Not in current catalog.

flaky_history.note: User-validated: 'surprisingly good'. Harness gave up at L2 ceiling but wizard kept running and produced a working artifact. Bump tier to L3 OR raise L2 timeout for game-class entries.

entries.goal: Missile Command arcade game. 6 cities at bottom of screen, 3 missile-launcher bases. ICBMs rain from top in an arc, player clicks to fire counter-missiles that explode in a radius, destroying ICBMs caught in blast. Lose a city when an ICBM reaches the bottom. Game over when all cities destroyed. Waves get harder (more ICBMs, faster descent). Score tracked.
entries.notes: Click-to-aim + explosion-radius + wave scaling. Simpler than Defender but still meaty. Novel territory.

Periodically individual enemies break formation and dive-attack the player. Boss Galaga can capture player ship (tractor beam); capturing a Galaga that has your ship gives you dual-ship bonus fire. Score tracked, wave progression.

entries.goal: Centipede arcade game. Mushroom-filled play field, player at bottom with 4-directional movement (constrained to bottom fifth of screen) and fire. Centipede enters from top, winds through mushrooms — when shot in the middle, splits into two centipedes. Spider periodically appears, destroys mushrooms, bonus when shot. entries.goal: Tempest-style tube shooter. First-person perspective looking down a geometric tube (rendered as wireframe lanes in 3D perspective). Player moves between adjacent lanes on the rim of the tube with arrow keys + fires down the lane. Enemies crawl up lanes toward player: flippers (flip between lanes), tankers (carry smaller enemies), spikers (leave spike traces). Clear each level by destroying all enemies. Vector-graphics aesthetic (wireframe on black bg).
entries.notes: Most ambitious entry — 3D perspective tube rendered on 2D canvas + vector-graphics style + multi-enemy-type. Great test of whether wizard can hold vector-graphics aesthetic across a complex 3D-ish render loop.

entries.goal: Plants vs Zombies-lite. Mobile-portrait 5-row × 9-column lawn. Sun falls at intervals (tap to collect). Plant cards in a horizontal strip at the BOTTOM of the screen — DRAG a card onto a lawn cell to plant it. Plant types: Sunflower (generates sun every 8s, costs 50), Peashooter (fires at zombies in its row, costs 100), Wall-Nut (high HP block, costs 50). Zombies enter from the right edge, walk left toward the house at the left edge — eat plants in their path. Game over when any zombie reaches the leftmost column. 3 increasing waves of zombies. Touch-only — no keyboard. Persist score + best-wave to localStorage. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules (mob-001 through mob-012).
entries.notes: Mobile-first canonical tower-defense. Tests drag-mechanic via Pointer Events + setPointerCapture (mob-011), thumb-reach control strip (mob-004), portrait viewport (mob-007). Major LLM challenge: holding the lawn-grid + plant-cost economy + zombie pathfinding + drag-to-plant in a single L3 turn budget.

flaky_history.note: User-validated 2026-04-25: visuals + layout look promising, BUT tapping the 'Play' button does nothing. Game-loop start is wired wrong OR the Play handler attaches before DOM is ready OR there's no transition from start screen to game state. Refine target.

entries.user_outcome: promising visuals but Play button doesn't start the game — refine candidate
entries.goal: Jetpack Joyride-style endless side-scroller, mobile portrait. The game world auto-scrolls horizontally; the player character is on the LEFT third of the screen. TAP-AND-HOLD anywhere on the screen makes the jetpack fire (ascend); release to fall. Avoid obstacles: stationary lasers (vertical bars), missiles (telegraphed by warning indicator at right edge for 1s, then fly across), zappers (rotating electrified bars). Collect coins arranged in arcs and lines. Distance counter at top, coin counter beside it. Game over on first collision. Persist best-distance + total-coins to localStorage. Touch-only, portrait. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom.
entries.notes: Tap-and-hold mechanic is the simplest mobile control — perfect L3 mobile entry. Tests viewport sizing (mob-001), tap-and-hold via pointerdown/up (mob-005), endless-scroll obstacle generator (procgen). Lower turn count than PvZ because the mechanic is simpler.

entries.goal: Candy Crush Saga-style match-3, mobile portrait. 8×8 grid of colored candies (5 colors). SWIPE a candy to swap it with an adjacent one — only legal if the swap creates a match-3 or longer in a row/column. Matches clear, candies above fall down, new candies fill from the top (gravity loop). Cascading matches multiply score. 30 moves per level — clear a target score in those moves to advance. 3 levels with increasing target. Special candies: 4-in-a-row makes a striped (clears whole row/col), 5-in-a-row makes a color-bomb (clears all of one color). Touch-only, portrait. Animation on swap, fall, and clear. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom.
entries.notes: Match-3 with gravity + cascade is hard. Wraps swipe-to-swap (Pointer Events, mob-011), grid math, animation timing, special-candy logic. Expect this one to need refines — flag if it ships with no cascade-detection or no special candies.

entries.user_outcome: PUBLISHED 2026-04-25 — solid first-pass, user shipped to production. (harness JSONL says timeout@300s but artifact was fully working — VER.Bug1 false-positive)
entries.goal: Flappy Bird-style mobile game. Portrait viewport. Tap anywhere to flap (small upward velocity impulse); gravity pulls the bird down constantly. Pipes scroll left at constant speed; player must fly through gaps. Each pipe cleared = +1 score. One collision = game over. Day/night cycle changes background every 10 pipes. Persist best-score to localStorage. Touch-only, portrait. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom.
entries.notes: Simplest possible touch game. L2 because the pipe-procgen + collision is real but contained. Good baseline — if flappy-085 fails, the mobile-frontend pack itself is suspect.

entries.user_outcome: PUBLISHED 2026-04-25 — solid, user shipped. (harness timeout@300s false-positive)
entries.goal: Slot machine, mobile portrait. 3 reels with 5 fruit symbols each (cherry, lemon, bell, bar, seven). Center 'SPIN' button takes 80% of screen width at the bottom (mob-004 thumb-reach). Tap SPIN: each reel shows a spinning blur for 1-3 seconds (each ending one after the other), then settles on a random symbol. 3 matching = win, payout based on symbol (cherry=2x, lemon=5x, bell=10x, bar=25x, seven=100x). Player starts with 100 credits, bets 1 per spin. Persist credits to localStorage. Haptic feedback on win. Touch-only.
entries.notes: Casual one-handed game. Tests haptic feedback (mob-009), thumb-reach control (mob-004), localStorage persistence (mob-012). Simpler than match-3 — should be a clean L2.

flaky_history.note: User-validated 2026-04-25: BAD. Real root cause via session inspection 2026-04-25T2125: Phase GC research path tried to learn 'idle-clicker' genre, research timed out, fell back to 'building from scratch with LLM internal knowledge'. WITHOUT genre research, fewer wisdom packs load and the artifact came out at only 4KB (vs 10-40KB for genre-matched mobile entries). Mobile pack DID partially load (viewport-fit, pointerdown, navigator.vibrate, localStorage all present) but missing 100dvh + safe-area + tap-highlight-color. Real fix is Phase GC: research-unavailable fallback should still load keyword-matched wisdom packs (universal + arcade-games + mobile-frontend), NOT 'build from scratch' (== no rules).

entries.user_outcome: BAD — tiny 4KB artifact. Phase GC research-fallback bug, not L1-fast-path wisdom-pack-loading issue. Filed for refine + Phase GC investigation.
entries.goal: Idle clicker, mobile portrait. Center: a giant tappable target (cookie/coin emoji or simple SVG circle), tapping it +1 to score. Below: an 'Auto-clickers' shop where score can be spent — Cursor (10 score = +1/sec), Grandma (100 = +5/sec), Factory (1000 = +20/sec). Score updates every 100ms from auto-clickers. Persist score + owned counts to localStorage. Haptic on each tap. Touch-only, portrait, no keyboard.
entries.notes: Casual idle game. L1 — simplest possible mobile-game pattern. Validates the mobile wisdom pack at the L1 fast-path tier (single API call). If this can't ship clean, the wisdom rules need tuning.

entries.user_outcome: PUBLISHED 2026-04-25 — works fine. (Status said failed in some surface but artifact was good — separate UX bug worth investigating; flagged.)
entries.goal: Tip calculator, mobile-first. Single-screen portrait. Top: large bill amount input (number pad opens on tap). Middle: row of tip-percent quick-pick chips (10/15/18/20/custom). Bottom: split-by stepper (1-10 people). All inputs flow into 3 large-format result rows: tip total, grand total, per-person amount. Each result row has a tap-to-copy button. Use 100dvh + safe-area insets so the keyboard doesn't push results off-screen. Touch-first.
entries.notes: Mobile-first utility (not a game). Tests viewport sizing under iOS keyboard (mob-003), thumb-reach result placement (mob-004), tap-to-copy (mob-005). Variant of catalog's existing tip-calc but with explicit mobile constraints — direct comparison candidate to see if 'mobile-first' steering changes the artifact.

entries.user_outcome: PUBLISHED 2026-04-25 — solid, user shipped. Mobile-first variant validated against desktop pomodoro — direct A/B win. (harness timeout@300s false-positive)
entries.goal: Pomodoro timer, mobile-first portrait. Top: massive countdown (font-size clamp 48-96px) showing MM:SS. Below: a single huge primary button (Start/Pause/Resume) — sized 80% screen-width so the user's thumb can't miss it. Below that: a smaller 'Skip' button. 25min work / 5min break cycle, automatic phase transition with strong visual + audio chime. Cycle counter at top. Persist current phase + remaining seconds to localStorage so a tab-switch doesn't lose progress (mob-012). Phase color changes (work=red, break=green). Touch-only.
entries.notes: Mobile-first productivity. Tests full mobile pack: viewport, thumb-reach, haptic+audio (audio gesture-unlock per mob-009), state-persist across tab-switch (mob-012). Variant of saturated 'pomodoro' theme but justified by mobile-specific constraints. Direct comparison candidate vs. desktop pomodoro generations.

entries.goal: Make me a mobile color-strobe / disco screen tool. Full-screen tappable area cycles through saturated colors. TAP to advance one color (Red→Orange→Yellow→Green→Cyan→Blue→Magenta→White → loop). LONG-PRESS to start auto-cycle (configurable speed via slider 0.2s-2s per color). DOUBLE-TAP to lock the current color (full-brightness solid). Brightness slider via opacity overlay. Portrait full-bleed, no chrome. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Replaces flashlight (no Wake Lock — relies on browser keeping screen on while user interacts) — pure CSS color strobe.

entries.goal: Build a mobile zen-circle drawing trainer tool. Big white canvas, draw a single closed circle with one continuous swipe (pointerdown → pointermove → pointerup). On release, score the roundness (variance from best-fit circle radius, 0-100 score). Show the user's stroke vs an overlaid 'perfect' circle. Best score persists. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Zen-circle ratings via least-squares circle fit. Continuous-pointer drawing test.

entries.goal: Coin flip web mini-app. Big circular coin in center showing HEADS or TAILS. TAP to flip — coin spins via CSS-3D rotateY transform for 800ms then settles on a random face. Tap & hold = 'extra wobble' (longer animation). Counter at top: H wins / T wins, persisted. Confetti burst on every flip. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Pure-CSS-3D flip animation. No DeviceMotion — tap is the only input.

entries.goal: Make me a mobile reaction-time game. Big START button. After random 1-3s delay, screen flashes green and a tap-target appears at random location — measure ms from flash to pointerdown. Show last + best reaction time. 'False start' if user taps during the wait period. Portrait, full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests precise input timing via performance.now() — common reaction-test mechanic.

entries.goal: Mobile stopwatch with lap times. Single big screen: huge MM:SS.mm readout (clamp font scaling). Two thumb-reachable buttons at the bottom: Start/Stop (left, primary action) + Lap/Reset (right). Lap button records current time to a scrollable list above the controls. Persist last 50 laps to localStorage. Portrait, full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Standard stopwatch but emphasizes thumb-reach + clamp-scaled font + lap persistence.

entries.goal: Fruit Ninja-lite. Fruits (apple/orange/banana/watermelon emoji on circular sprites) toss up from the bottom in groups of 1-4 at random arcs. Drag-swipe across the screen to slice them — each slice plays a satisfying particle-burst (split halves fly apart with physics). Bombs occasionally toss up — slicing them ends the game. Combo multiplier for slicing 3+ fruits in one continuous swipe. 60s timer. Portrait, full-bleed. Persist best score. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests continuous-pointermove drag with setPointerCapture, projectile physics, slice-detection geometry.

entries.goal: Doodle Jump-lite web. Character auto-bounces up between procedurally-generated platforms scrolling top-to-bottom. DRAG horizontally on the bottom 30% of the screen to steer (relative offset, not absolute) — finger movement = horizontal velocity. Screen wraps horizontally (off left edge → reappears right). Game over if you fall off the bottom. Distance-meter at top. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Bottom-drag steering replaces tilt. Wrap-around screen geometry + procedural platforms.

entries.goal: Piano Tiles-lite. 4 vertical lanes, black tiles scroll downward at increasing speed, tap each tile when it reaches the bottom-strike-zone (44px-tall band). Missing or tapping a white tile = game over. WebAudio: each tile plays a short piano note from a C-major pentatonic. Combo + score top-of-screen. Tile speed accelerates every 10 taps. Persist best score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Rhythm tap-game with strict 44px touch targets (mob-002) + WebAudio per-tile note + ramping difficulty.

entries.goal: 2048 mobile-touch. 4x4 grid, swipe in 4 directions to slide+merge tiles. SWIPE direction = pointerdown→pointerup vector (no on-screen buttons). Animate tile slide + merge with CSS transitions. Show next-best-tile preview top-right. Persist best score + current board to localStorage. Portrait, full-bleed (bottom-edge insets respected). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: 2048 IS in the saturated list but mobile-swipe-only variant is novel.
entries.notes: Pure-swipe 2048, no button chrome. Tests pointer-vector → direction inference + persistent state across reloads.

entries.goal: Helix Jump-lite. Vertical helix tower with rotating colored ring-platforms (some have gaps). Ball auto-falls — DRAG horizontally to rotate the entire tower and align the gap with the ball's column. Ball passes through gap = +1 level + camera pans down. Ball lands on a non-gap = bounce, OR on the same color twice = game over. 8+ levels with varying gap counts. Visual flash on level-clear. Portrait, full-bleed. Persist best level. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: 3D-feel via 2D rotation. Drag-to-rotate-world-not-character is a novel mobile gesture.

entries.goal: Mobile quick-note. Big bottom-thumb-zone textarea (autofocus, 60% screen height, 18px font). Above it: 6 most-recent notes as horizontally scrolling chip cards (200px wide, swipe to delete with red action sheet). Tap a chip to load it back into the textarea. Auto-save on every keystroke (debounced 300ms) to localStorage. Long-press a chip = pin to top. Search bar at very top (filters chips live). Portrait, full-bleed (with safe-area insets). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests bottom-thumb-zone primary-input ergonomics + horizontal-scroll chip pattern + long-press contextual menu.

entries.goal: Mobile grocery list with swipe-to-complete. Big add-input at the bottom (autofocus on item add). List items scroll above. SWIPE-LEFT on an item to mark it bought (slide-out animation, item moves to a collapsed 'bought' section at the bottom). SWIPE-RIGHT to delete (fade + remove). Tap item to edit inline. Categorize via emoji prefix (auto-suggest 🥦 for veggies, 🥩 for meat, 🥛 for dairy from a small built-in dictionary). Persist to localStorage. Pull-down-to-add-from-recent-history (last 50 unique items as quick-tap chips). Portrait, full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Swipe-left vs swipe-right disambiguation + emoji auto-categorize + pull-down-to-show-recents.

entries.goal: Mobile finger-paint canvas. Full-screen canvas (100dvh, background white). Drag finger to paint with smooth bezier strokes (pointer interpolation between samples for smoothness at high velocities). Bottom strip: 8 color swatches (44px each, mob-002), brush-size slider (2-30px), undo button, clear button, save-to-photo button (uses canvas.toBlob → download .png). Pinch-to-zoom on canvas (two-finger pointers). Long-press a color = open hex-input modal. Portrait, full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tests multi-pointer (pinch-zoom), bezier path smoothing, and toBlob download. Stretch: undo stack.

entries.goal: Stack-the-tower mobile game. A horizontal block slides back and forth across the screen at the top of the current tower. TAP to drop it — it lands on the existing top block. Any portion that hangs off the edge gets sliced off (the next block is narrower). Stack as high as you can before a block becomes too narrow to land. Camera pans up as tower grows. Color palette shifts hue per block. Visual flash on perfect stack (within 5px alignment = bonus + tower DOESN'T narrow). Portrait, full-bleed. Persist best height. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Single-tap timing-game with perfect-stack reward mechanic + camera scrolling. Hue-shift palette is delightful detail.

entries.goal: Color Switch-lite. A small ball auto-falls due to gravity. TAP anywhere to make the ball jump up (impulse). Below: a vertical sequence of rotating obstacles (rings, crosses, squares, half-circles), each split into 4 colored segments. The ball is always one of those 4 colors — only passes through obstacle segments matching its color. Color-switcher pickups change the ball's color. Camera pans up as you climb. Game over if ball falls off bottom. Portrait full-bleed. Persist best height. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Color-aware collision detection + rotating multi-color obstacles + 1-tap input. Major challenge: clean rotating-obstacle hit-testing.

entries.goal: Temple Run-lite endless runner. Pseudo-3D corridor (perspective-skewed lanes, NOT actual WebGL — use scaled 2D layers). 3 lanes. Character auto-runs forward. SWIPE LEFT / RIGHT to change lane. SWIPE UP = jump (clears low obstacles). SWIPE DOWN = slide (clears high obstacles). Coins to collect, obstacles to dodge, distance counter, increasing speed. Game over on collision. Visual flash on coin grab + crash. Persist best distance + total coins. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: 4-direction swipe disambiguation + pseudo-3D rendering + endless procedural-spawn pattern. Hard L3.

entries.goal: Crossy Road-lite. Top-down chunky pixel character. SWIPE in 4 directions to hop ONE GRID CELL at a time. Cross alternating lanes: roads with cars (don't get hit), rivers with logs (must land on a log or you drown), grass (safe). Camera auto-scrolls upward — if you fall behind off the bottom edge, game over. Procedurally spawn lanes ahead. Score = highest row reached. Portrait full-bleed. Visual flash on death + every 25 rows. Persist best. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Discrete grid + 4-way swipe-hop + multi-rule lane types (cars/logs/safe) + auto-scroll death gate. L3 because of procedural lane gen + collision logic + log-riding edge cases.
entries.user_outcome_note: 2026-04-25 user feedback: "Hopper was pretty good!" Shipped as Hop Quest, 37KB, 5/9 mobile-pack tokens.

entries.goal: Frogger mobile. Frog at bottom, must cross 5 lanes of traffic + a river with logs to reach the top safely. SWIPE to hop one cell in any direction. River requires landing on a log (rides with it). Off the screen with the log = drown. Frog spawns are placed at the top safe-zone (5 home slots — fill all 5 = bonus level). Timer per crossing. Portrait full-bleed. Visual flash on death. Persist best level + total frogs home. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Classic Frogger but mobile-touch + with home-slot fill mechanic. Tests 4-way swipe + log-rider physics + per-level timer.

entries.goal: Bejeweled-lite mobile match-3 (different from Candy Crush — emphasis on chains + power gems). 8x8 grid of gems (6 shapes). DRAG-swap two adjacent gems to make a 3+ match. 4-match → makes a flame gem (clears entire row/column on next match). 5-match → makes a hyper-cube (tap to clear all of one color). Cascades fall + auto-match. Score-based level progression (every 1500 pts = level up = faster spawn). Persist best score + level. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Match-3 with power-gem creation + cascades. Different from CandyCrush (which was levels-based) — this is endless-mode classic Bejeweled.

entries.goal: Mobile music pad — 4x4 grid of colored beat pads. TAP each pad to toggle a step in a 16-step sequencer (current row). 4 rows = 4 instruments (kick, snare, hat, perc). BPM slider at top (60-180). Play/Stop button + clear-row button. Pads light up as the playhead crosses them. WebAudio: programmatic drum sounds (simple oscillators + noise + envelopes). Save 4 pattern slots to localStorage. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: 16-step grid sequencer + WebAudio synth. Tests mob-009 (resume AudioContext on first tap) + sample-accurate scheduling. Major LLM challenge: clean envelope shapes + low-CPU-overhead playback.

entries.goal: macOS menubar Pomodoro timer. Click the menubar icon → small popover with Start/Pause/Reset. Single 25-min cycle, tomato emoji countdown text in the menubar item itself. NO break cycle, NO settings — just one timer. Native SwiftUI + NSStatusItem.
entries.notes: Avoids the saturated 'pomodoro' miniApp territory by being native + menubar-resident — exercises NSStatusItem button-text updates which menubar-clock-071 didn't (clock just opens a popover; this one mutates the bar item text every second).

entries.goal: macOS one-shot UUID generator. Single 400×200 window. Big 'Generate UUID' button → creates a UUIDv4, displays it in a monospaced text field, copies to NSPasteboard automatically. History list of last 5 UUIDs below. No persistence between launches.
entries.notes: Tests NSPasteboard auto-write (asciiart-074 tests it but on user click; this one fires automatically). Common dev-utility surface.

entries.goal: macOS battery percentage menubar app. Reads system battery level via IOPowerSources / IOPSCopyPowerSourcesInfo, shows percentage in the menubar item (e.g. '87%'). Click → popover with charging status + estimated time remaining. Updates every 60s.
entries.notes: Tests system-info APIs (IOKit) which previous menubar entries don't. Fragile target for the LLM — IOPS C-API bridging is non-trivial.

entries.goal: macOS link-stash app. Single-window list view of saved URLs with title + favicon (auto-fetched). Add via paste-button or paste-detect on launch. Persists to ~/Library/Application Support/LinkStash/links.json. Click row → opens in default browser via NSWorkspace. No tags, no folders, just a chronological stash.
entries.notes: First L2 macOSApp. Exercises persistence + URLSession (favicon fetch) + NSWorkspace.open. No reliance on third-party Swift packages.

entries.goal: macOS color palette builder. Two-pane layout: left = list of saved palettes, right = active palette with up to 8 ColorPicker swatches. Add/remove swatches. Each palette has a name. Persists palettes to JSON in Application Support. Export current palette as a Swift `Color` array snippet to clipboard.
entries.notes: Tests NavigationSplitView (macOS-native two-pane) which color-contrast-073 doesn't. Exercises Codable round-trip on Color (via hex string conversion).

entries.goal: macOS clipboard history menubar app. NSPasteboard polling every 1s; when a new string copy is detected, prepend to a 50-item ring buffer. Menubar popover shows the last 10. Click any row to copy back. Esc dismisses. Persists buffer across launches via UserDefaults JSON.
entries.notes: Common macOS power-user app. Polling NSPasteboard.changeCount is the canonical pattern; tests the LLM knows it instead of busy-reading the string each tick.

entries.goal: macOS tip calculator window app. Bill amount TextField (currency formatter), tip-percent slider (10-30%), party-size stepper. Live-updates show: tip total, per-person, grand total. Save the last-used percent + party size to UserDefaults so they persist across launches. Single 480×320 window.
entries.notes: Common finance tutorial-app subject — exercises currency formatting + multi-input live binding. Saturated as miniApp but novel as native macOS window.

entries.goal: macOS multi-window markdown scratchpad. Each window = one note with a live-rendering preview pane (split view, drag to resize). File menu: New, Open .md, Save / Save As (NSSavePanel), Export as HTML. Notes auto-save to ~/Library/Application Support/Scratchpad/<uuid>.md every 2s. Dock-shown app, recent-files menu populated. Multiple windows simultaneously.
entries.notes: First L3 macOSApp — tests CLI-agentic Meta.W path (planner + CLI generation). Multi-window via WindowGroup/DocumentGroup is the canonical macOS pattern that L1 fast-path can't reach. Phase WX-A's dist/*.app recovery should fire here.

entries.goal: Mobile anagram word game. 6 letter tiles in a horizontal strip — DRAG-rearrange them to form a valid English word (3-6 letters). Tap-and-hold a letter to lock it in place. SHUFFLE button (bottom-right thumb zone) randomizes letter order. New 6-letter set every 60s; goal is to find as many words as possible. Bundled JSON of common English words (~3000). Persist best score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Drag-rearrange + tap-and-hold-to-lock + shuffle button (replacing shake). Pure pointer events.

entries.goal: Mobile Hangman with on-screen keyboard. Hidden word at top (under blanks). Custom 7×4 letter-keyboard at the bottom (in thumb-reach, 44px+ targets). Tap a letter — if in word, fills blanks; if not, draws next limb. 6 wrong guesses = game over. Categories: animals, foods, places, sports (chosen at start via 4-button modal). Persist win-streak + best-streak. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: hangman IS in saturated list but mobile-touch keyboard variant + category-pick is novel.
entries.notes: Novel mobile UX for classic hangman — keyboard ergonomics is the bar.

entries.goal: Letter Blast — mobile word puzzle. 5x5 grid of letter tiles. Tap-and-DRAG a path through adjacent tiles (orthogonal+diagonal) to spell a word ≥3 letters. Path lights up as you drag; release to commit. Used letters refill from above with gravity. 90-second round, score = sum of (length²) per word. Bonus: 5+ letter words trigger 'BLAST' that clears 3-tile radius. Persist best round score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Boggle/Wordscapes hybrid — drag-path word selection + gravity refill + radius-blast bonus.

entries.goal: Mobile rhyme race. Show 1 source word at top (e.g. 'CAT'). Below, 6 word-button options — tap any that rhyme. 60s round, +1 point per correct, -1 per wrong. New source word every 4s. Categories: 'easy 3-letter', 'medium 4-5 letter', 'hard 5+'. Use a small bundled rhyme-lookup JSON (curated 200 source words → rhyme arrays). Visual flash on correct. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Quick-fire rhyming for kids/wordplay. Tests bundled-data lookup + tight per-tap timing.

entries.goal: Mobile mini-crossword (5x5 daily). Standard crossword grid with black squares + numbered clues. Tap a cell to start typing — virtual keyboard slides up from bottom (custom, full-width, 44px+ keys). Auto-advance to next cell, auto-jump on word complete. Across/Down clue list scrollable above keyboard, current clue highlighted. Cell-by-cell auto-check (green=correct, red=wrong) toggleable. Persist current puzzle state + completion stats. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Crossword UX is a classic-mobile-design challenge: virtual keyboard + clue/grid layout in 375px-wide portrait. Single bundled puzzle as L3 demo.

entries.goal: Klondike Solitaire mobile. 7-tableau-column layout fits portrait via columns 1-7 in two rows (or compact horizontal scroll). Cards are small (45x65px) but tap-target ≥44px via overlay. DRAG card-or-card-stack from one column to another. Auto-flip top of column on uncover. Foundation piles top-right. Stock + waste top-left. Tap stock to deal 3. Persist current game + best-time + win-count. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: solitaire IS in saturated list but cards-on-portrait-mobile is non-trivial layout challenge.
entries.notes: Hard layout problem: 7 tableau columns at ~52px/card-width + drag-stacks. Tests setPointerCapture + portrait constraint creativity.

entries.goal: Blackjack mobile. Dealer hand top, player hand bottom. 4 huge thumb-zone buttons: HIT, STAND, DOUBLE, SPLIT (44+ px each, bottom 25% per mob-004). Card sliding-deal animation. Bet sizing: tap chip-stack to add ($5/$25/$100). Bankroll persists. Animations on win (golden flash + vibrate). Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: blackjack IS saturated; this variant emphasizes mobile-touch ergonomics + bankroll-persistence.
entries.notes: Tests thumb-zone primary-action layout + bet-chip-stack interaction + bankroll-survival. mob-004 / mob-005 / mob-009.

entries.goal: Uno mobile (vs 3 AI). Bottom of screen: your 7-card fan, scrollable horizontally. Center: discard pile + draw deck. Top: 3 AI opponents with card-counts. Tap a card to play (if legal — match color/number/wild); if illegal, vibrate + flash red. SWIPE-up on a card to play it; SWIPE-down to draw. AI auto-plays in 1s intervals. Wild-card → modal with 4 color buttons. Persist win-rate. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Multi-card hand UI on portrait + swipe-play vs swipe-draw + simple AI. Hard L3 challenge: card legality engine + AI heuristics.

entries.goal: Memory match-pairs mobile. 4×6 card grid (24 cards = 12 pairs). TAP card to flip. Match 2 = stays face-up; mismatch = both flip back after 1s. Timer + move-counter. Themes: emojis (animals/foods/sports/transport — pick at start). Visual flash on match. Confetti + win-time on full-clear. Persist best-time-per-theme. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: memory IS saturated; novel mobile addition is theme-pick + per-theme leaderboard.
entries.notes: 4×6 grid in portrait must be tested — 80px cards. Mobile-pack should reinforce thumb-target sizing.

entries.goal: Mobile mini-deckbuilder roguelike. 1-card-per-turn combat: bottom hand has 5 cards drawn from your deck (15 cards total, expandable). TAP to play; cards have cost (energy 0-3) + effect (attack/block/heal). Enemy at top has HP/intent indicator. After each fight, choose 1 of 3 reward cards to add to your deck. 5-fight run, then boss. Mid-run save. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Slay-the-Spire-mini. Tests turn-based state machine + persistent deck + simple enemy AI + portrait card-row layout.

entries.goal: Tic-Tac-Toe with twist: 4×4 'connect-4-in-a-line' instead of 3×3 (more interesting, fits portrait). Two-player local: tap to place X/O. Or vs-AI mode (minimax depth-3). Big cells (≥80px). Win-line animates with golden trail. Visual flash on win. Persist W-L-D record + per-mode stats. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Connect-4-in-line variant (4x4 grid, line=4) is significantly more strategic than 3x3 + works on portrait. Minimax AI as L2 stretch.

entries.goal: Dots and Boxes mobile. 6×6 dot grid. TAP between two adjacent dots to draw a line. When you complete the 4th side of a square, you claim it (filled with your color) and get another turn. Vs-AI greedy strategy. Score = boxes claimed. Visual flash on box-claim. Portrait full-bleed. Persist W-L-D. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Pencil-and-paper classic. Tap between dots is a tight hit-zone challenge — 44px overlay around each potential edge.

entries.goal: Mobile Checkers (American Checkers / Draughts). 8×8 board. TAP your piece to select; valid-move squares highlight green. TAP a green square to move. Captures auto-chained (multi-jump). King promotion on opponent's back row. Vs-AI alpha-beta depth-4. Portrait: board fills width, controls + score below. Persist record + best-streak. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tap-select-then-tap-target is the standard board-game UX. Hard L3 because of valid-move highlighting + multi-jump chain + alpha-beta AI.

entries.goal: Mancala mobile. 6 pits per side + 2 stores at the ends. TAP one of your pits to scoop & sow. End-store rules apply (sow into your own store but skip opponent's). Free-turn rule for landing in your store; capture rule for landing in your empty pit opposite a non-empty enemy pit. Vs-AI heuristic player. Score = stones in your store. Portrait full-bleed (board oriented vertically — pits on left/right columns, stores top/bottom). Persist record. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Vertical-board reorientation for portrait is the design challenge. Mancala rules are fiddly — tests rule-engine correctness.

entries.goal: Pac-Man mobile. Top-down maze (12×16 cells, fits portrait) with dots, power-pellets, 4 ghosts. SWIPE in 4 directions to set Pac-Man's next-move-direction (queued — moves at next intersection). Eat all dots = level clear. Power-pellet → ghosts edible (10s). Score + lives top-of-screen. Persist best score per level. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Swipe-queue direction (not on-screen joystick) is the mobile innovation. Tests maze pathfinding + ghost AI states + portrait-fit maze geometry.

entries.goal: Breakout/Arkanoid mobile. Bricks at top, paddle at bottom, ball bounces. DRAG horizontally on the bottom 30% of screen to move paddle (relative — finger position offset, not absolute). TAP anywhere = launch ball. Power-ups fall from broken bricks (multi-ball, wider paddle, lasers). 5+ levels. Persist high score. Portrait full-bleed (taller-than-wide = more bricks, longer fall). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: breakout IS saturated; mobile portrait + relative drag-paddle is novel.
entries.notes: Relative-position paddle drag (not absolute) is a critical mobile-touch UX choice. Tests setPointerCapture for paddle that can drift off-finger.

entries.goal: Modern Snake mobile. SWIPE in 4 directions to turn (queued at-grid-cell). Snake grows when eating food. Power-foods: golden (slow-mo for 5s), red (multi-grow), blue (ghost-mode pass through walls 3s). Walls wrap or kill (toggle). Endless mode + timed mode. Persist best length per mode. Portrait full-bleed (taller = more snake-length headroom). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: snake IS saturated; mobile + power-foods + mode-pick is the novel angle.
entries.notes: Swipe-queue directional input matches Pac-Man entry — pattern-tests are useful. Power-food state-machines are the L3 stretch.

entries.goal: Mini-golf mobile (top-down). 9 holes, each a single screen (no scrolling). Tap-and-DRAG from ball backwards = aim line + power (length of drag). Release = strike. Ball physics with friction + wall bounces. Obstacles: walls, sand (slows), water (death-reset). Scorecard tracks strokes per hole. Persist best per hole. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Drag-back-to-aim is canonical mobile-golf UX. Tests vector-physics + collision + 9-level state.

entries.goal: Pinball web mini-app (portrait one-screen). Two flippers at bottom — TAP LEFT half of screen = left flipper, TAP RIGHT half = right flipper. Ball physics. Bumpers + ramps + slingshots score points. Multi-ball power-up. PLUNGER strip at bottom: drag-down-to-pull-back-and-release for ball launch with adjustable power. Persist high score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Tap-half-of-screen flippers + drag-plunger replace tilt-nudge. Pure pointer pinball.

entries.goal: Mobile tower defense (different from PvZ — more like Bloons-TD). Curving path through scenic level, enemies walk along it. DRAG towers from a bottom-strip onto the map (any non-path location). 4 tower types (single-target, AoE, slow, money-gen) each with 3 upgrade levels. Money earned per kill. 10 increasing waves of enemies (basic / fast / armored / boss). Persist best wave + currency. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Path-based TD is structurally different from grid-based PvZ — tests path-following enemy AI + drag-place-tower-anywhere + tower-upgrade UI.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Candy Crush × Wordle. 6×6 grid of letter-tiles (frosted candy aesthetic). DRAG-swap two adjacent tiles to make a 3+ word horizontally OR vertically (must spell a real English word). Words clear + cascade with gravity. 5-letter word = makes a 'wild' wildcard tile. 6-letter = clears entire row. Score-based level progression. Bundled common-words JSON (~3000 entries). Persist best score + level. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Sweet Cascade (Candy Crush) scored 6/9 mobile tokens + user said 'is great!' Word-puzzle adds intellectual depth. Combines drag-swap mechanic + word-validity engine. Probably the most novel of the bunch.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Plants vs Zombies × Chess. 8×8 chess board. White = Plants (pawn=Sunflower, rook=Wallnut, bishop=Peashooter, knight=Cherry-bomb, queen=Cattail, king=House). Black = Zombies (mirrored — pawns are basics, etc). Standard chess movement BUT: capture happens via BOTH pieces fighting (HP-based instead of one-shot — peashooter fires till one dies in same turn). King = the House: zombies must reach + survive 1 turn. Drag-piece-to-square mobile UX. Vs-AI minimax depth-3. Persist W-L-D. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: PvZ scored 9/9 mobile tokens (best). Chess strategic depth + PvZ characters is genuinely fresh. HP-based capture is the novel rule-twist that breaks chess monotony.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: 2048 × Flappy Bird. Side-scrolling endless. Bird auto-scrolls right; pipes have NUMBERED gaps (powers of 2: 2, 4, 8, 16). Bird shows current number. TAP to flap up. Pass through a pipe matching your number = number doubles + score. Wrong pipe = collision. Game over on hitting wall/floor/wrong-pipe. Persist best score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Flappy ('Solid!') + 2048 — the merge-doubling intellectual hook adds strategy to pure-twitch flappy. Choice of which pipe to aim for IS the gameplay.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Jetpack Joyride × Bejeweled. Side-scroll endless runner BUT the world is a vertical 6-column gem-cascade. Jetpack character on the left edge auto-scrolls (no horizontal movement). HOLD to fly UP, RELEASE to fall, dodging gem-walls. Touching matching gems collects them — collect 3+ same-color in a row = combo. Periodic 'gem rain' levels are full match-3 puzzle screens (drag-swap to clear). Portrait full-bleed. Persist distance + total gems. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: JetPack ('promising') + Bejeweled — adds collection-strategy to twitch-runner. Periodic puzzle-screens break up pure-physics flow.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Tetris × Snake. Standard tetris well (10×20). Tetrominos fall. CONTROL = a 'snake' that grows along the bottom — it can absorb dropped blocks into its body (instead of stacking) by slithering under them. Eating blocks = score; stacking blocks = lose like normal tetris. Snake DIES if rammed by stacked blocks. Persist best score. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules. NOTE: tetris IS saturated but the snake-absorbs-blocks twist is genuinely novel.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Snake + Tetris — both 4-direction-input games, both grid-based. Snake-absorbs-tetrominos is fresh — turns enemy (falling block) into food.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Pomodoro × Idle RPG. Standard pomodoro (25min work, 5min break). Each completed work session = your character ATTACKS a fantasy monster (boss has HP scaled to expected hours). Each break = your character HEALS or shops. Defeat monster = level-up + new ability + harder monster. Stats: HP, ATK, gold. Inventory persists. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Pomodoro ('Solid!') + idle-RPG — gamifying work sessions with persistent character progression is a famous productivity hack (Habitica, Forest). Mobile fits — phone is where focus-trackers live.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Doodle Jump × 2048 (web). Vertical endless platformer — character auto-bounces. Platforms have NUMBERS (2, 4, 8, 16, 32...). When you land on a platform with the same number as your character → both double (you absorb its number). Land on different → just bounce. DRAG horizontally on the bottom 30% of screen to steer (relative offset). Goal: highest single-number reached + total height. Power-ups: number-doublers, halvers. Persist best. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: Bottom-drag steering replaces tilt. Bouncing-merge game.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Tip Calculator × Fitness Tracker. Restaurant bill calculator that ALSO computes calorie-burn-equivalent. Enter bill amount + cuisine type (8 cuisines mapped to avg cal-density). Calc tip + per-person split AS USUAL, but also show: 'This meal = X minutes of running / Y minutes of walking / Z burpees'. Saves last 30 meals to localStorage with timestamp + cal estimate. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Tip-calc (4/9 mobile-tokens) + health-awareness. Goes from 'utility' to 'utility with surprising value-add.' Cuisine→cal-density mapping is teaching-by-doing for users.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Temple Run × Typing Test. Runner auto-runs forward in pseudo-3D corridor. Obstacles ahead show WORDS — type the word (mobile soft-keyboard at bottom) before reaching obstacle to vault over it. Long words = bigger obstacles. Mistype = collision. Coins from successful types. Persist WPM + best distance. Portrait full-bleed (full-screen kbd takes bottom 40%). Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Temple Run (new) + typing-test (saturated). Endless-runner gives stakes to typing speed — the 'why' that pure typing tests lack.

entries.goal: MASH-UP: Bejeweled × Piano Tiles. Vertical 4-column piano grid scrolling DOWN with colored gem-tiles instead of black tiles. Tap each tile when in strike zone. Tap 3+ same-color gems consecutively = MELODY combo (extra score + WebAudio plays an arpeggio). Tap-and-hold a gem = 'sustain' note. Mistakes break combo. Increasing tempo. Persist best score per song. Portrait full-bleed. Apply mobile-frontend wisdom rules.
entries.notes: MASH-UP RATIONALE: Bejeweled-color-matching + Piano Tiles rhythm-tapping. Color-combos create melodies — every successful play sounds musically pleasant. Tests WebAudio note scheduling + combo state machine.

entries.goal: macOS Mahjong tile-match solo game. SwiftUI Canvas-rendered grid of 36 tiles in a 6x6 layout (subset of full mahjong). Click two tiles with matching glyphs (use SF Symbols like sun, moon, leaf, drop, flame, sparkles for the 6 tile types) to remove the pair. Match all 18 pairs to win. Single window 600x600. Cmd+S to save current layout to ~/Library/Application Support/Mahjong/layout.json via NSSavePanel; Cmd+O to load.
entries.notes: First macOSApp game entry. Native-mac angle: actual Save/Open file panels exporting layouts as JSON. Mahjong solitaire is absent from the saturated miniApp list. ~150 LOC target. Bumped L1→L2 on 2026-04-25 after Mahjong L1 canary failed: NSSavePanel sequences need planner stage, which L1 fast-path skips. See gotchas_l1_macos_native_specifics.md.

entries.goal: macOS menubar Desk Pet idle clicker. Lives entirely in NSStatusItem — emoji or SF Symbol shows current pet mood (😀 fed, 😴 hungry, 💤 starving). Click the menubar icon → NSPopover with: pet status, hunger meter (0-100, decreases 1 point/min), big 'Feed' button (resets hunger to 100), 'Stats' line ('Fed N times'). Persists via UserDefaults across launches. No main window — menubar-only.
entries.notes: Idle game that only works as a native menubar app — cannot be a web mini-app. Tests NSStatusItem-only apps (no main window) which previous menubar entries don't. ~180 LOC.

entries.goal: macOS asteroids-style game using SpriteKit + native GameController support. Player ship rotates with arrow keys (or left stick on a connected gamepad), thrust with up arrow / right trigger, fires with space / X button. Asteroids spawn at edges, drift toward center, break into smaller pieces when shot. Score top-left, lives top-right. Use SpriteKit physics (SKPhysicsBody) for collisions. Wrap a SKView in NSViewRepresentable inside a SwiftUI App scene. Auto-detect connected gamepads via GCController.controllers().
entries.notes: First SpriteKit entry. Native-mac angle: GameController gives extended profile + rumble that web games can't reach. Asteroids variants OK per user spec (not on saturated list). ~400 LOC across 3-4 files.

entries.goal: macOS Whack-a-Mole game with native high-score export. SpriteKit scene with a 3x3 grid of holes; moles pop up from random holes for 1-2 seconds. Click a mole to score; miss = -1. 60-second timer per round. After Game Over, an alert with a 'Save High Score' button — opens NSSavePanel to write a high-scores.json file the user can put in iCloud Drive or anywhere. File menu: 'New Game' (Cmd+N), 'Restart' (Cmd+R), 'Show Scores' (Cmd+H). Single 600x600 window.
entries.notes: Native-mac angle: real file-system high score export via NSSavePanel — not a localStorage hack. Tests CommandMenu (macOS native menus) which no other entry covers. ~300 LOC. Confirmed L2 is the right tier — NSSavePanel call sequence needs planner stage.

entries.goal: macOS tower defense game 'Dock Defender'. Path of enemy 'Finder windows' walks across the screen toward an icon at the right edge (your Dock, represented as a SwiftUI shape). Player places towers (3 types: Slow tower, Damage tower, Splash tower) on grid cells along the path. Towers auto-fire at enemies in range. Right-click a placed tower → NSMenu with Upgrade / Sell options. Use GameplayKit's GKGraph for enemy pathfinding through a grid. SpriteKit for animation/rendering. 5 waves, increasing difficulty. Currency earned per kill, spent on towers.
entries.notes: First L3 macOSApp game. Tower defense is the Phase GC reference genre — fits the dynamic-genre pipeline. Native-mac distinction: right-click NSMenu on towers (Mac-native context menu, not a custom popover). Exercises CLI-agentic Meta.W path because L3. ~1100 LOC target.

entries.goal: macOS turn-based roguelike dungeon crawler. SwiftUI Canvas-rendered ASCII grid (8x8 dungeon, generated procedurally with GameplayKit's GKARC4RandomSource for seedable randomness). Player ('@') moves with arrow keys; walls ('#'), enemies ('g' goblin, 'r' rat), items ('!' potion, '$' gold). Step on enemy = melee combat; turn-based. Goal: reach the down-stairs ('>') on each level (3 levels). Cmd+1-5 = quick-save slots, each saving full dungeon state to ~/Library/Application Support/Roguelike/save{N}.json. Menubar shows current dungeon depth. CommandMenu has 'New Game', 'Quit to Menu'.
entries.notes: Second L3 macOSApp game. Native-mac angle: Cmd+1-5 quick-save slots via CommandGroup — Mac-native modifier+number shortcuts mapped to file-system slots. No third-party deps; all GameplayKit + SwiftUI. Bounded scope (8x8, 3 levels) to keep within L3 budget. ~1300 LOC target.

entries.goal: macOS soundboard app. Single 480x360 window with a 3x3 grid of pad buttons. Each button labeled with an emoji + sound name (e.g. 🥁 'Drum', 🔔 'Bell', 👏 'Clap', 🚃 'Train', 🐮 'Cow', 🎷 'Sax', 🗲 'Magic', 🚂 'Whistle', ⚡ 'Zap'). Tapping a pad plays a short tone via AVAudioPlayer reading from sounds bundled in the SwiftPM resources directory (Sources/Soundboard/Resources/*.wav). Use AVAudioSession-free direct AVAudioPlayer init (macOS doesn't need AVAudioSession). 1-9 keyboard shortcuts trigger the matching pad. Visible "Now playing" footer text shows the most-recently-triggered pad name; clears after 1.5s. No mute/volume; just tap-and-play.
entries.notes: First AVFoundation entry in macOSApp catalog. Native-mac surface = Bundle.module SwiftPM resource path (Package.swift `resources: [.copy("Resources")]`) — common-but-trickier than embedding sounds as base64. The LLM is free to either ship 9 actual .wav files via the resources directory OR generate them inline with an AVAudioEngine sample-buffer write at first launch. Either is acceptable; the test is whether it knows AVAudioPlayer needs a file-on-disk URL on macOS, not a bundled stream.

entries.goal: macOS QuickLook thumbnailer. 500x420 window, drag-target area at top showing 'Drop any file here' (NSDraggingDestination). Drop a file (PDF, image, video, doc — anything QuickLook handles) → app calls QLThumbnailGenerator.shared.generateBestRepresentation with size 256x256, scale = NSScreen.main.backingScaleFactor, type = .thumbnail. Result CGImage rendered in the lower half. Below it, a 'Save Thumbnail…' button opens NSSavePanel filtered to .png and writes the rendered thumbnail as PNG via NSBitmapImageRep. Show source filename + 'Thumbnail generated in N ms' caption. Errors (e.g. encrypted file) replace caption with red error text. No sandbox entitlements needed beyond the dropped file's security-scoped URL.
entries.notes: First QuickLookThumbnailing entry. Surfaces drag-IN (NSDraggingDestination via SwiftUI's .onDrop with .fileURL UTType) + a real macOS framework most LLMs know exists but rarely emit correctly. Native-mac angle: backing-scale-factor handling for retina output (LLM forgetting this gives a half-resolution PNG). Bonus mile: NSSavePanel with allowedContentTypes filter (UTType.png) is the modern path; old-school .allowedFileTypes is deprecated.
